options.why: Truncated instead of rounding — kept the digit and dropped the rest.
Left the number unchanged; did not round to one decimal place.
Rounded to the nearest whole number instead of one decimal place.
Rounded up even though the next digit ($4$) is less than $5$.
Rounded to one decimal place instead of two.
Did not round at all — left all three decimal places.
Rounded the tenths digit up without carrying the extra one into the ones column.
Made a place-value slip, carrying the digit into the wrong column.
Rounded up to the next half instead of the next tenth.

questions.q: A number rounds to $6.2$ (one decimal place) and has two decimal places. What is the **largest** value it could have been?

options.why: Gave the upper boundary itself — $6.25$ rounds up to $6.3$, not $6.2$.
Gave the rounded value itself, not the largest value that still rounds to it.
Gave a value near the lower bound instead of the largest possible value.
